summary_text: 1. Publication-integrity risk: accidental move from `draft` to schedule/publish. Validation gate: freeze mode first (`draft only` / `approved for scheduler` / `manual immediate publish`) and block any publish path unless explicitly requested. 2. Source-grounding risk: generated claims not anchored to the Forbes article. Validation gate: extract quote evidence first (key facts, numbers, chronology), then allow only claims that map to those quotes; label anything else as inference or remove it. 3. Claim-verification gap risk: dates/stats/entities can drift or be misstated. Validation gate: require claim-by-claim check for all concrete facts (numbers, dates, named actions) against extracted source text before draft acceptance. 4. Leakage risk (repo invariant): brand/person/product names leaking into `Body`/`Body JA` or thumbnail text without explicit approval. Validation gate: run proper-noun leakage check across `Body`, `Body JA`, and image text; if user did not explicitly approve, generalize to neutral language and keep specifics in `Source URLs` only. 5. URL-mode risk: mixing lead-post vs quote/reply rules. Validation gate: decide mode up front; for quote/reply flow, body must contain no `x.com`/`twitter.com` URL and reference URL must live in `Source URLs`. 6. Schedulability risk: draft length/style not safe for x-auto queue. Validation gate: if this is a schedulable candidate, enforce runtime-safe `800-1000字` target; if shorter, mark explicitly as non-posting ideation draft. 7. Operational sync risk: text is drafted but Notion/x-auto state is inconsistent. Validation gate: before handoff, verify row-field completeness and consistency (`Body`, `Body JA`, `Source URLs`, `Image`, `Status`) and treat Notion row read as canonical.
